strategy.name: VWAP + RSI(3) + EMA(8) Scalping Strategy — Enhanced
strategy.description: Seven-layer filter stack. Original three-indicator signal (EMA8 trend, VWAP session bias, RSI3 entry timing) gated by six additional filters: Claude macro regime, volume confirmation, multi-timeframe alignment, trading hours, ATR position sizing, and correlation control.

indicators.ema_8: Trend direction. Price above EMA(8) = bullish momentum. Below = bearish.
indicators.vwap: Session bias. Price above VWAP = buyers in control. Below = sellers. Resets at midnight UTC. Falls back to last 6 candles if session has no data yet.
indicators.rsi_3: Entry timing. RSI on 3 candles only — extremely sensitive. Below 30 in an uptrend = snap-back setup. Above 70 in a downtrend = reversal likely.
indicators.atr_14: Volatility measure used for position sizing. Position size = 1% portfolio risk / (ATR x 1.5 stop multiplier), capped at MAX_TRADE_SIZE_USD.

filter_stack.description: All filters must pass before any order is placed. Filters are applied in sequence — early failures skip remaining checks.

time_filter.rule: No trades between 01:00–05:59 UTC — exception: RSI(3) > 80 or RSI(3) < 20 overrides the block
time_filter.reason: Lowest liquidity window globally. Wide spreads, high slippage risk, thin order books. Strong RSI signals (extreme overbought/oversold) are allowed through as the edge outweighs the liquidity risk.

volume_confirmation.rule: Current candle volume must be >= 60% of the 20-candle average
volume_confirmation.reason: Low volume signals disinterest. Entries on thin volume produce worse fills and higher slippage. Threshold lowered from 80% to 60% to allow trading during normal post-breakout consolidation without including genuine dead markets.

multi_timeframe.rule: 1H bias must be directionally clear (bullish or bearish) and align with 4H signal
multi_timeframe.reason: Filters out counter-trend traps. A 4H signal against a neutral 1H is unreliable.

correlation_filter.rule: Do not trade BTCUSDT and ETHUSDT in the same bot run
correlation_filter.reason: BTC and ETH are highly correlated. Trading both simultaneously doubles exposure without adding independent edge.

claude_regime_filter.rule: Claude (claude-haiku) evaluates macro environment before every trade. Blocks during: Fed/CPI/NFP announcements, extreme sentiment divergence, black swan events.
claude_regime_filter.reason: Technical signals cannot account for macro shock risk. Claude reads real-world context that indicators cannot.

long: Price is above VWAP — buyers in control for the session
Price is above EMA(8) — uptrend confirmed
RSI(3) has dropped below 30 — short-term pullback in an uptrend, snap-back likely
short: Price is below VWAP — sellers in control
Price is below EMA(8) — downtrend confirmed
RSI(3) has spiked above 70 — overbought in a downtrend, reversal likely

exit_rules: Exit when RSI(3) crosses back above 50 (for longs) or below 50 (for shorts)
Hard stop: 0.3% below entry for longs, 0.3% above entry for shorts
Take profit at next VWAP touch or EMA(8) cross
risk_rules: Risk maximum 1% of portfolio per trade (ATR-based sizing)
Hard cap: no single trade exceeds MAX_TRADE_SIZE_USD
Hard cap: no more than MAX_TRADES_PER_DAY trades per calendar day
Do not trade if price is more than 1.5% away from VWAP — overextended
Do not trade BTC and ETH in the same run — correlation doubles risk
Do not trade during 01:00–05:59 UTC — low liquidity window
Do not trade on volume below 80% of 20-candle average
Claude regime filter blocks trades during macro events
notes: 4H timeframe primary. RSI(3) is intentionally fast and sensitive — it fires quickly and exits quickly. Claude regime filter uses claude-haiku for speed and cost efficiency. Bot runs every 30 minutes to catch RSI spikes and volume threshold crossings faster. All decisions logged to safety-check-log.json. All trades (blocked or executed) logged to trades.csv.
